fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Indiana Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Indiana - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity indiana, how to transfer physical therapist license to indiana, physical therapist compact indiana, indiana physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Indiana PLA — Physical Therapy Committee
board.mailing_address:
Indiana Professional Licensing Agency
402 W Washington Street, Room W072
Indianapolis, IN 46204

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years, expiring December 31 of even-numbered years

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Indiana.

steps.title: Review Application Requirements
steps.description: Visit the Indiana Board of Physical Therapy website to understand the specific requirements for licensure by examination (new graduates) or by endorsement (licensed in another state).

steps.title: Create MyLicense E-Gov Account
steps.description: Access the MyLicense E-Gov portal (mylicense.in.gov/E-Gov/Login.aspx) and register for a new user account. You will need your last name, SSN/TIN, and date of birth.

steps.title: Complete Online Application and Pay Fee
steps.description: Log into your MyLicense E-Gov account, select the appropriate application (e.g., 'PT by Examination' or 'PT by Endorsement'), complete all sections, and pay the non-refundable $100 application fee.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for all required official documents, such as academic transcripts and NPTE scores (if by examination), or license verifications from other states (if by endorsement), to be sent directly to the Indiana Professional Licensing Agency.

steps.title: Pass the Indiana Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Instructions for registering and completing the Indiana Jurisprudence Examination will be provided after your online application has been submitted and reviewed by the agency.

steps.title: Await Board Review and Licensure
steps.description: Monitor the status of your application through your MyLicense E-Gov account. The agency will review complete applications, and some may require Board approval before your license is issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Indiana physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Indiana have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Indiana participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Indiana without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Indiana?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Indiana while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Indiana
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Indiana, you must submit your application and supporting documents online through the MyLicense E-Gov portal.

variants.label: Renewal

official_links.registration_steps: 1. Go to the MyLicense E-Gov portal and click 'Register a New User Account'. 2. Enter your last name, SSN/TIN, and date of birth. 3. Follow the prompts to create a username and password. 4. Log in to begin your application.
